methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Earth AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Infrastructure & Logistics), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Amperon is a Series B infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $20M to date. The comparable universe for this sector is sparse, consisting of two known private peers (AiDash and Earth AI) with no public comparables identified. The limited peer set constrains traditional multiple-based valuation approaches.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, using the $20M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression. This method yields an implied valuation of $38M. A peer-median approach was not applied due to insufficient comparable density—only one same-sector peer exists, falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for reliable median derivation. The sparse comp universe materially limits triangulation robustness.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $32M (low) to $44M (high), with a midpoint of $38M. This range reflects primarily the stage-progression method, which carries inherent uncertainty given the single-method basis. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier for Amperon's specific growth trajectory, the limited peer visibility in the Infrastructure & Logistics sector, and the absence of public market anchors. Any valuation exercise for this company would benefit materially from expanded comparable data or transaction precedents within the sector.
